description: Figure 9 presents the relationship between deformation microstructure and flow stress in Fe35MnxAl0.1C low-density steels. Panel (a) plots the normalized shear stress (τ-τ0)/G/10^-3 versus the normalized inverse spacing b/D/10^-3 for the austenite phase in 4Al steel, yielding similitude constants K_DC = 2.02 (dislocation cells, gray squares and black line) and K_SB+DCB = 2.03 (slip bands plus dislocation cell blocks, red circles and red line). These K values fall close to the reported range of 2.0–2.9 for austenitic stainless steels, and because the austenite phases in 4Al and 8Al steels share similar compositional systems and deformation structures, the constants obtained from 4Al are used to approximate the austenite behavior in the duplex 8Al steel. Panel (b) compares the measured true stress (black squares and line) with the calculated total flow stress σ_0+σ_α(ε)+σ_γ(ε) (red squares and line) for 8Al steel at true strains of approximately 0.025, 0.10, 0.175, and 0.25. The stacked columns decompose the calculated stress into the base stress σ_0 (~455 MPa, orange), the ferrite contribution σ_α(ε) (green), and the austenite contribution σ_γ(ε) (purple). Both phase contributions increase with strain, with σ_α dominating the strain hardening, and the close agreement between calculated and measured stresses validates the composite flow stress model based on dynamic slip band refinement in ferrite and dislocation cell evolution in austenite.
